key_insight: A multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization workflow can tune genetic algorithm hyperparameters so a short 25-generation run matches the mechanical performance of a much longer 75-generation search.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost lattice material design by reducing computational time while preserving elastic modulus performance.
method: High-fidelity FFT homogenization used to validate candidate lattice designs.
Medium-fidelity 3D CNN surrogate provided rapid property estimates.
Low-fidelity GP-based Bayesian optimization guided GA hyperparameter search; logNEI performed best.
evidence_signal: High-fidelity validation showed optimized settings cut total runtime by 24% (225 to 171 hours) while maintaining comparable elastic modulus.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on surrogate accuracy and noisy GA evaluations, so transfer to new lattice families may require revalidation.
paper_title: Bayesian Optimization of Genetic Algorithm Hyperparameters in a Multi-Fidelity Framework for Efficient Lattice Material Design
